body.title: Executor routing + the closing pass
body.statement: CLOSING PASS (Akien's standing practice): before /sorted files a design block, ask 'What am I missing? What could we do better?' and record the concrete answers — additions, corrections, or an explicit 'nothing else' with reasoning. EXECUTOR ROUTING (current): CC (Claude Code) authors all diffs; Granny routes tickets by role to available builders over the bus (handshake, never spawn); rack builders (DS/aider tier) take mechanical builder-tier tickets when live; HIGH-inertia diffs stay with CC.0 under inline Akien pre-approval (unseenuniversity/rules/safeguards). Igor is retired from coding (2026-05) — any 'Igor handles MEDIUM and LOW diffs' text is stale and must not route work.
body.why: The two closing questions reliably surface gaps while they cost a sentence instead of a sprint. Routing by role keeps the expensive resolver on judgment work — the whole point of the substrate — and dead routing targets (Igor-as-coder) send tickets into a void.
sources: builder feedback: Igor coding retired 2026-05; CC owns all work

validity_conditions.target: Igor is retired from authoring code tickets
validity_conditions.note: if an Igor coding loop is re-approved, the routing half of this rule is stale
